title: AWS
purpose: Compose cloud compute, storage, networking and managed services securely.
outcomes: Explain AWS concepts from first principles
Choose when to use and when to avoid important AWS features
Implement, test and debug practical AWS solutions
Answer conceptual, internal-working and production interview questions

stages.title: Fundamentals
stages.objective: Build an accurate mental model of AWS before relying on frameworks or memorized answers.

topics.title: Regions, availability zones and IAM
topics.definition: Regions, availability zones and IAM is a fundamentals part of AWS concerned with how engineers deliver and operate software repeatably across infrastructure. It provides a vocabulary, operating model and constraints for making that part of a system deliberate rather than accidental.
topics.whyItMatters: Without understanding Regions, availability zones and IAM, a AWS solution may work on the happy path but become difficult to change, debug or operate. This topic makes ownership, behavior and failure conditions explicit.
whenToUse: Use Regions, availability zones and IAM when it directly supports a required AWS behavior
Use it after identifying the input, output, constraints and failure policy
Use it when the team can test and operate the resulting behavior
whenNotToUse: Do not add Regions, availability zones and IAM only because it is popular or appears in an architecture diagram
Avoid it when platform complexity exceeds the reliability or scale benefit
Avoid abstraction before the repeated problem and ownership boundary are understood
internalWorking: Input enters the Regions, availability zones and IAM boundary and is validated against its contract
Declarative configuration and controllers move runtime resources toward a desired state
State changes should preserve a named invariant and produce an observable result
Failures propagate through a defined error, retry or recovery path
tradeoffs: Correctness and clarity versus implementation effort
Runtime performance versus memory or operational complexity
Flexibility versus a smaller, easier-to-test contract
commonMistakes: Memorizing the definition of Regions, availability zones and IAM without tracing an example
Ignoring invalid input, failure and cleanup behavior
Choosing an advanced implementation before measuring the simpler one
topics.practicalExample: In the placement platform, apply Regions, availability zones and IAM to one AWS workflow such as creating an assessment, loading study content or calculating results. Define the contract, implement the happy path, reject invalid input and record enough context to diagnose failure.
